Petition For Approval Of Executor Carol Leigh Szego?S Resignation
Off Calendar Feb-08-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vanessa Sundin, via Zoom, for Thomas Lefrak, Petitioner.

The court and Ms. Sundin discuss Ms. Szego's Petition to resign as Executor which is set for hearing on Feb-08-2023. The court believes that Ms. Szego's petition is not required as the court can accept her resignation on the record and appoint the Petitioner successor Executor. The court confirms that Ms. Szego has waived an accounting within the petition. Ms. Sundin reports on the ongoing class action litigation and is hopeful that the matter will be resolved by sometime next year and the plan is to keep this Estate open until that matter is resolved. After additional discussion, the court takes judicial notice of Ms. Szego's pending petition for resignation and accepts her resignation on the record. Accordingly, the court grants the Petition for Letters Testamentary. The Will dated Jun-19-2016 was previously admitted by order of the court on Sep-14-2015. Richard Lefrak is appointed successor Executor with full authority and no bond. Shirley Yawitz remains probate referee. Status on Close of Estate is set for Nov-21-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

The Petition for Approval of Executor Carol L. Szego's Resignation scheduled for Feb-08-2023 is taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Vanessa Sundin, Esq., counsel for Carol Szego, Executor (appearing telephonically).

The Court reports on review of the accounting, noting that certain fees claimed as extraordinary appear to be statutory. Ms. Sundin responses, generally advising the Court of certain activities under the special administration. The Court queries counsel on the status of the mesothelioma litigation. Ms. Sundin advises that the litigation is approaching the end and that she expects a minimal distribution, if any, due to the bankruptcy of certain entities.

The Court takes the current petition under submission and sets the matter for future review on August 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-27-2020 Continued To Aug-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Vanessa Sundin appeared by telephone for Carol Szego.

The Court stated that it is concerned about how the request for extraordinary fees fits into the statutory fees that will be awarded. Ms. Sundin stated that she filed the time sheets on Friday, but it hasn't posted, that the statutory compensation allowed to the firm under the Probate Code is 27,525.38 and that it is insufficient to cover the amount of time spent performing services. The Court noted that the assigned Probate Referee has retired. Ms. Sundin stated that her office appears to be in the process of requesting appointment of another Probate Referee. The Fifth Report on Status of Administration of Executor on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to August 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for review of the time sheets.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
